description: Citation-quality benchmark eval set (ALCE-style). Each case supplies a query plus the GROUND-TRUTH verified claims (with supporting spans) that a good answer should assert and cite. The harness seeds these as verified citations, lets a real model draft + cite, then scores citation recall (did claim-bearing sentences get cited?) and precision (do citations actually support their sentence?).

cases.query: Will antibiotics help cure my common cold? I keep hearing they don't work on viruses.

verified_claims.claim_text: Antibiotics do not treat viral infections.

sources.title: WHO — Antibiotic resistance
sources.supporting_span: Antibiotics are used to prevent and treat bacterial infections; they do not work against viral infections such as colds and flu.

verified_claims.claim_text: The common cold is caused by viruses, not bacteria.

sources.title: CDC — Common Cold
sources.supporting_span: Most colds are caused by rhinoviruses; the common cold is a viral infection of the upper respiratory tract.

verified_claims.claim_text: Misusing antibiotics for viral infections contributes to antibiotic resistance.

sources.title: CDC — Antibiotic Use
sources.supporting_span: Taking antibiotics when they are not needed accelerates the development of antibiotic resistance.

cases.query: Is there a link between childhood vaccines and autism?

sources.title: CDC — Vaccines and Autism
sources.supporting_span: Studies have shown that there is no link between receiving vaccines and developing autism spectrum disorder.

verified_claims.claim_text: The 1998 study claiming a vaccine-autism link was retracted for fraud.

sources.title: BMJ — Wakefield's article an elaborate fraud
sources.supporting_span: The Lancet paper has been retracted; the journal found the research to be fraudulent.

cases.query: Can you really see the Great Wall of China from space with the naked eye?

verified_claims.claim_text: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.

sources.title: NASA — Great Wall
sources.supporting_span: The Great Wall is generally not visible to the unaided eye from low Earth orbit; the claim that it is the only human structure visible from space is a myth.

cases.query: Is Pluto still classified as a planet?

sources.title: IAU — Pluto reclassification
sources.supporting_span: In 2006 the IAU defined a new category, 'dwarf planet', and reclassified Pluto as a dwarf planet rather than a planet.

verified_claims.claim_text: A planet must clear the neighborhood around its orbit under the IAU definition.

sources.title: IAU — Planet definition
sources.supporting_span: A planet is a celestial body that orbits the Sun, is round due to its own gravity, and has cleared the neighbourhood around its orbit.

cases.query: Does water always boil at 100 degrees Celsius?

verified_claims.claim_text: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.

sources.title: NIST — Boiling point
sources.supporting_span: At one standard atmosphere (101.325 kPa) the boiling point of water is 100 degrees Celsius.

verified_claims.claim_text: Water boils at a lower temperature at higher altitude due to reduced pressure.

sources.title: USGS — Boiling and altitude
sources.supporting_span: Because atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude, water boils at temperatures below 100 degrees Celsius at high elevation.

cases.query: Is it true that goldfish only have a three-second memory?

verified_claims.claim_text: Goldfish have only a three-second memory.

sources.title: Scientific American — Goldfish memory
sources.supporting_span: Research shows goldfish can remember things for weeks or months; the 'three-second memory' is a myth.
